## What is DNS and how does it relate to VPNs?

DNS basics meet VPN privacy so your lookups stay private while you browse



### What DNS does (and why it exists)

Every time you visit a website, your device needs directions to find it. That's where DNS (Domain Name System) comes in. It translates web addresses (like expressvpn.com) into the numerical IP addresses computers use to connect.

By default, your network uses your ISP's DNS servers, which can log your browsing activity. [ExpressVPN's privacy-focused features](https://www.expressvpn.com/features) are designed to reduce that visibility and give you more control over how your data is handled.



### DNS vs. VPN: What's the difference?

DNS servers and VPNs serve different purposes. A DNS translates website URLs into IP addresses, like looking up a phone number in a directory. A VPN [encrypts your internet traffic](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/vpn-encryption) and masks your IP address, protecting your data from snooping.

The catch? Most VPNs protect your traffic but leave DNS queries unencrypted or exposed. A VPN running its own private DNS on every server keeps your DNS lookups encrypted within the same secure tunnel.



### What happens to DNS lookups when you use a VPN

How does DNS work with a VPN? It varies by provider. Best case, [your VPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn) encrypts your DNS queries and routes them through its own private DNS servers. Your ISP and network admins see only encrypted traffic.

Worst case, your VPN uses external DNS providers or fails to encrypt DNS queries. This creates a DNS leak, exposing which sites you visit to your ISP or DNS provider; even while your VPN is connected.



### Can you use a custom DNS with a VPN?

Some VPNs let you configure a custom DNS, but the best VPNs use their own private DNS servers by default for stronger privacy and leak protection. This built-in option is usually optimized for speed, security, and reliability.

If you set up a custom DNS, follow the VPN configuration guidelines to maintain protection, and use ExpressVPN's [DNS leak test](https://www.expressvpn.com/dns-leak-test) to verify your connection stays secure.

[Try ExpressVPN's DNS Servers](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)



## Why use a VPN with a private DNS?

ExpressVPN combines private DNS servers with VPN protection for end-to-end privacy



### Enhanced privacy and fewer intermediaries

Without a private DNS VPN, your requests travel through third-party servers, exposing your browsing to ISPs, hackers, and advertisers. Some VPNs use external DNS providers that can log or sell your activity, defeating the purpose of using a VPN.

ExpressVPN's private DNS encrypts and resolves requests internally through zero-knowledge servers, eliminating intermediaries that could log, leak, or monetize your data.



### Reduced DNS query exposure

A private DNS encrypts every domain lookup, blocking snooping and surveillance at the source. With ExpressVPN's DNS, every request is securely handled within our encrypted infrastructure, keeping your browsing private from end to end.

This layered protection helps to eliminate weak points that could expose your data or activity. Which makes it much harder for outsiders, even on monitored networks like in corporate environments, to analyze your online activity.



### Protection against DNS tampering and blocking

Some networks may hijack or spoof DNS requests, redirecting you to fake or unsafe pages. Imagine logging in to your banking app or your emails, only to end up on a suspicious site or see a strange error.

By handling all DNS requests on ExpressVPN's secure servers, you'll reach the legitimate site you intended with added encryption. That means less frustration, fewer security risks, and greater confidence that your connection remains private and protected.



### Speed and reliability benefits

ExpressVPN's private DNS lookups aren't just secure, they're fast. DNS queries are handled on our optimized [servers across 113 countries](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-server), reducing delays from third-party routing and avoiding the potential congestion that comes with public DNS servers.

Combined with built-in redundancy and our low-latency [Lightway protocol](https://www.expressvpn.com/lightway), you get fewer hiccups, faster page loads, and reliable streaming.

## FAQs

How DNS works with a VPN: All you need to know



### What is the difference between DNS and VPN?

The DNS process translates website names into IP addresses while a VPN encrypts all your internet traffic and hides your IP address. The DNS process alone doesn't protect your privacy, while a VPN offers full data encryption and IP masking for secure, private browsing.


### How does DNS behave when using a VPN?

When using a VPN, DNS requests are routed through the VPN tunnel to the VPN's own DNS servers, as reputable providers do to prevent leaks, rather than your ISP's. This encrypts your queries and prevents your ISP from tracking which websites you visit, keeping your browsing activity private.


### Can I use custom DNS with ExpressVPN?

When connected to ExpressVPN, all DNS queries automatically use ExpressVPN's private DNS servers for maximum security. This prevents DNS leaks and third-party exposure. You can configure custom DNS settings on your device, but they only apply when you're disconnected from ExpressVPN.


### What is 1.1.1.1, and how does it compare to VPN DNS?

1.1.1.1 is Cloudflare's public DNS server address. While fast, it exposes your DNS queries to Cloudflare, creating a third-party privacy risk. A VPN's DNS encrypts queries inside the VPN tunnel with no external exposure. ExpressVPN's private DNS eliminates the need to trust multiple providers with your data.


### Is 208.67.222.222 safe to use as a DNS server?

208.67.222.222 is OpenDNS's server address—safe from security threats but not private. Using it exposes your DNS queries to Cisco, who can log your activity. ExpressVPN's private DNS encrypts queries within our tunnel with zero third-party access and an independently audited no-logs policy ensuring complete privacy.


### What is the fastest DNS server for VPN users?

The fastest DNS server for VPN users is usually the VPN's own private DNS. ExpressVPN uses a private, zero-knowledge DNS on every server, ensuring fast lookups, minimal latency, and complete privacy—no third parties, no logs, no leaks—so your connection stays both fast and secure.


### Does ExpressVPN log or route my DNS traffic externally?

No. ExpressVPN does not log DNS requests and handles all DNS queries internally through its private servers, ensuring no external routing or logging of your DNS traffic.


### How does ExpressVPN prevent DNS leaks?

ExpressVPN prevents DNS leaks by routing all DNS queries inside the encrypted VPN tunnel, blocking any DNS requests from leaking. This built-in leak protection safeguards your privacy on every connection.


### How can you test for DNS leaks?

Use ExpressVPN's DNS leak test page to check if your DNS queries are securely routed through VPN servers. If you identify a leak, contact our 24/7 live support staff for assistance.


### What are a VPN's custom DNS settings?

VPN custom DNS settings let you choose which DNS servers your VPN uses instead of the default ones. With ExpressVPN, you don't need to change anything. Every server already uses private, zero-knowledge DNS for maximum speed, privacy, and leak protection, so your connection stays fast, secure, and fully encrypted by default.

## Fast VPN protocols

Your connection could get faster than ever when you use ExpressVPN’s fast [VPN protocols](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/protocols)! ExpressVPN offers several protocol options, including:

[Lightway](https://www.expressvpn.com/lightway)** - UDP**, which we built from the ground up to meet the needs of modern internet users. Offering fast speeds, high reliability, and industry-leading security, Lightway is only available on ExpressVPN.

**Lightway - TCP** might be slower than UDP but connects better on certain networks. This allows Lightway to be used in a wide range of scenarios.

[OpenVPN ](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/protocols/openvpn)**- UDP **offers a strong combination of speed and security but it may not be available on every network.

**OpenVPN - TCP** may be slightly slower than UDP but is available on almost every network.

[IKEv2](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/protocols/ikev2), which is secure and well-suited for [mobile devices](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-mobile), but slower than OpenVPN and Lightway.

ExpressVPN automatically chooses the best protocol option based on your network, but you also have the option of choosing between options manually.

## Built on privacy-first technology

ExpressVPN’s privacy-first tech safeguards your traffic data from the ground up



### TrustedServer infrastructure and regular audits

Traditional servers store data on hard drives, which can leave information behind. ExpressVPN’s [TrustedServer technology](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/trustedserver) ensures all servers run entirely on RAM so no activity data is stored, and wipes all servers regularly to load each server’s operating system with a fresh version.

ExpressVPN’s privacy protections, including our [no-logs policy](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/policy-towards-logs), are independently audited by firms such as PwC and Cure53 to confirm that they operate as designed.



### ExpressVPN’s Lightway protocol for reliable speed

Network changes and traffic spikes cause VPN slowdowns: buffering, lag, dropped connections. [Lightway](https://www.expressvpn.com/lightway)’s minimalist design enables faster processing and instant reconnection when you switch from Wi-Fi to cellular.

Walk from your home Wi-Fi to your car without your VPN session dropping. Lightway keeps connections responsive whether you're stationary or moving between networks throughout your day.


### Based in a privacy-friendly jurisdiction

ExpressVPN is based in the British Virgin Islands, outside the 5/9/14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliances. This means it isn’t subject to data-retention laws and isn’t required to collect, store, or share user activity.

That legal foundation supports our strict no-logs policy and ongoing privacy work, including research into post-quantum protections designed to address future threats. Our [Trust Center](https://www.expressvpn.com/trust) explains these commitments transparently.

## What ISP throttling is and why it happens

There’s a few reasons your internet provider may reduce your speeds



### Network congestion and peak usage

Fed up with slow internet in the evenings? When lots of people in your area are online at once, the ISP network gets crowded. Your internet provider may slow some connections to keep things stable for everyone. If streaming or gaming always drags at peak hours but feels fine at other times, congestion is a likely culprit.​



### Data caps and bandwidth limits

Are your speeds slowing down even though your connection seems fine? Many plans have data caps or “fair use” limits, and once you hit them your provider can slow down your connection. HD streaming, large downloads, or using multiple devices can mean you hit these limits quickly, causing throttling or interruptions.​


### Heavy usage or high-demand services

Your connection might slow to a crawl when you stream, game, or download huge files. Some providers target these high-demand activities and slow them down while leaving lighter tasks, like browsing, mostly untouched. That’s why one app might constantly buffer while others feel normal.​ This is commonly known as content-based throttling.



### Traffic prioritization by providers

ISPs can prioritise certain types of traffic—like voice calls or system updates—to keep them reliable during busy periods. In some cases, they may also prioritise content from providers that pay for better delivery. When that happens, everyday activities like browsing or streaming can end up feeling slower. This is another form of content-based prioritization (also called content-based throttling).

[Try ExpressVPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)

Help reduce interruptions to your online accounts with a VPN



### Internet providers may slow your speeds based on your activity

Have you noticed games lagging or videos pause, even when other apps seem fine? Your internet provider may slow down high-bandwidth activities (like downloads) to help manage the network load.

This content-based throttling helps keep the overall network stable, but it can make your connection uneven. So browsing might stay quick, but your games stutter and streams struggle.



### Throttling can disrupt streaming, gaming, and video calls

Interruptions can ruin an experience. The frozen screen during the key scene, missing the crucial shot in a game, or a call that glitches when you’re trying to connect with loved ones.

The good news? This type of content-based throttling, where certain apps or activities get slowed down, is something you can avoid with an ISP throttling VPN.


### How ExpressVPN helps prevent content-based throttling

With [ExpressVPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn), your internet traffic is encrypted and your [ IP address is hidden](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-my-ip/hide-my-ip). That means your ISP can’t see what you’re doing online and won’t be able to throttle your connection based on your activity.*

The result? You experience more consistent speeds during peak hours and when using apps that would normally get lower ISP speed prioritization. Plus stronger privacy and security every time you go online.

**ExpressVPN can help with content-based throttling, but it can’t prevent slowdowns caused by data caps, overall network congestion, or throttling that applies to all traffic.*

## How to tell if your ISP is throttling you

It’s easy to check whether your ISP is throttling your connection


- Test your internet speed

Use a speed-testing site or app to check your connection at different times of day, or even on different days. If speeds drop noticeably during peak hours, like evenings, it could be throttling. Compare the results to the speeds promised in your internet plan.
- Compare your speed with a VPN

Connect to a VPN and run the tests again. If your speeds improve significantly, your ISP may be throttling your traffic. Using a VPN hides your activity with encryption, so your provider can’t slow certain types of traffic, even at peak hours.

## Frequently asked questions

Find out more about how to stop ISP throttling based on activity



### What is ISP throttling?

ISP throttling is when your provider intentionally slows your connection. Content-based throttling is when the throttling happens based on what you’re doing online, such as streaming or gaming. ExpressVPN helps prevent this by encrypting your traffic, making it harder for providers to identify specific activities and apply these targeted slowdowns.


### How do I know if my internet is being throttled?

Check if your internet is being throttled by testing your speeds using an online speed test tool or simply searching "what is my internet speed". If you notice a significant increase compared to testing when you're connected to a VPN, it's possible your ISP is throttling your speeds.


### Is ISP throttling legal?

Yes, it's legal for providers in many regions to manage traffic with throttling on their networks. Using a VPN can help you avoid content-based throttling, giving you a more consistent experience regardless of what you’re doing online.


### Does throttling affect mobile data?

Mobile carriers can throttle data just like home broadband providers, especially during congestion or after you hit usage limits. ExpressVPN works across mobile networks and encrypts your traffic, helping reduce activity-based slowdowns.


### Can free VPNs prevent ISP throttling?

Free VPNs often lack strong encryption, reliable servers, and the high-bandwidth networks needed to reduce throttling. ExpressVPN offers a global network of 40Gbps servers and the fast Lightway protocol for more consistent speeds and enhanced security.

# Stay protected with ExpressVPN Internet Kill Switch

Internet Kill Switch automatically blocks all internet traffic until the secure VPN tunnel is restored


- Keep your data from leaking through connection drops and switching networks
- Protect your privacy on every device connected to ExpressVPN
- Try the optimized Lightway protocol for lightning-fast reconnection

## Why use a VPN kill switch

Keep your information safe, even on unreliable connections


- Stop data leaks during connection drops

When a [VPN service](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn) disconnects because your laptop is in sleep mode, you switched Wi-Fi, or a hotspot dropped, your device can send traffic outside the encrypted tunnel.
A VPN kill switch halts that traffic until a secure connection is restored, keeping your data secure.
- Prevent IP and DNS exposure on shaky networks

Even a minor network glitch can expose your DNS request to your ISP, or your IP to others on the network and other third parties.
A kill switch closes that window by blocking all non-VPN traffic until the VPN is online—something a simple auto-reconnect can’t do on its own.
- Stay private without constant checks

VPN automatic kill switch reconnection is a common feature, but you may still experience small bursts of unprotected data.
A true kill switch adds an extra layer of protection, blocking unencrypted traffic even before auto-reconnect kicks in.

[Try ExpressVPN Risk-Free](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)



## What Internet Kill Switch does to keep you safe online

Learn how a VPN kill switch works to provide instant protection for your data



### Prevents data exposure during VPN drops

When the VPN disconnects, Internet Kill Switch applies firewall-based rules that block all unencrypted traffic.

This containment covers IPv4, IPv6, and DNS requests until the encrypted tunnel is back online, upon which traffic resumes automatically.



### Maintains secure connections across networks

Internet Kill Switch stays on, even when switching between networks on Windows, macOS, and Linux, maintaining protection without user input.

It works side-by-side with ExpressVPN’s proprietary [Lightway protocol](https://www.expressvpn.com/lightway) for fast, stable reconnections to quickly restore the encrypted tunnel.

This combination keeps your connection secure during Wi-Fi changes or short signal drops, so your data never travels outside the VPN’s encrypted tunnel.



## How to turn on Internet Kill Switch in ExpressVPN



### Windows, Mac, and Linux

On Windows, Mac, and Linux, Internet Kill Switch is on by default and can be turned off with a few clicks. While enabled, it will block your internet traffic automatically if the connection is interrupted. This proactive measure is an essential safeguard against network disruptions and prevents any unencrypted traffic from leaking out. When the VPN connection is back up, you’ll be unblocked and back in action in moments thanks to Lightway.



### Android and iOS

On Android, ExpressVPN offers two layers of protection. The in-app kill switch is enabled by default and blocks internet traffic if the VPN disconnects, while still allowing [split tunneling](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/split-tunneling) and access to local devices. For stricter protection, Android system settings can block all traffic that isn’t routed through the VPN, even after you disconnect.

**On iOS**, open *Settings* and go to *Internet Kill Switch*, choose *Standard* or *Advanced Kill Switch* to stop traffic when the VPN disconnects.

- Windows, macOS, and Linux

- Routers

Internet Kill Switch is permanently active on [routers with ExpressVPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-router), giving every device connected to the network the same level of protection.
It helps keep smart TVs, game consoles, and all other devices on your network that can’t run VPN apps directly secure. You can also exclude devices via split-tunneling.


- Android

On [Android](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-android), the kill switch blocks internet traffic if the VPN disconnects unexpectedly, while split tunneling access remains untouched.
Need stricter control? You can enable *Block connections without VPN* in Android settings to block all traffic, including devices on the local network.
- iOS

On iOS, you’ll find two kill switch options. The *Standard Kill Switch* blocks internet traffic only if the VPN disconnects for any reason.
The *Advanced Kill Switch* blocks *all* network activity when the VPN is off, except for essential Apple DNS requests. This stops any unencrypted data from leaving your device without VPN protection.

## FAQs: About VPN kill switches

Discover the answers to your most common questions about a VPN kill switch



### What is a VPN kill switch?

A VPN kill switch, also called a VPN automatic kill switch, blocks web traffic if your internet connection drops. ExpressVPN’s Internet Kill Switch does this at the system level to keep your IP address and data protected inside the encrypted tunnel. The VPN restores your connection when it reconnects.


### Should you enable the kill switch?

Absolutely. Keeping your VPN kill switch on helps you prevent leaks if your connection drops. Internet Kill Switch is on by default on Windows, macOS, and Linux, with one-tap protection on Android and iOS. Lightway supports rapid reconnection, so there's no need to worry about the kill switch interrupting your browsing.


### How do you activate the kill switch in ExpressVPN?

On desktop and mobile, navigate to the *Profile* > *Settings*. Under Internet Kill Switch, you can choose how it works: block traffic only when the VPN is connected, or enable "at all times" to keep traffic blocked whenever the VPN is off.
On Aircove, the Internet Kill Switch is always on, so you don’t need to change any settings.


### What if Internet Kill Switch isn't working correctly?

If Internet Kill Switch isn’t blocking traffic when the VPN connection is interrupted, make sure your ExpressVPN app is up to date. Re-enable the feature under Settings › General or contact ExpressVPN support for a quick checkup by our team.


### Does every VPN offer a kill switch feature?

Not every VPN provides a full system-level VPN kill switch. Some need manual activation or protect only specific apps. ExpressVPN’s Internet Kill Switch blocks IPv4, IPv6, and DNS traffic outside the encrypted tunnel on Windows, Mac, Linux, iOS, and Android devices.


### How can I tell if my kill switch is active?

Want to know how to test a VPN kill switch? Connect to ExpressVPN, then turn off your internet connection briefly. If your internet pauses until Lightway restores the tunnel, the kill switch is active.

## Why use VPN split tunneling?

Take control of your traffic with a split tunneling VPN

Have you ever noticed your printer, smart TV, or certain apps stop working when you’re connected to a VPN? Some banking apps may even show loading errors because they block VPN traffic.

Split tunneling can prevent these potential issues by letting you choose which apps and websites use the VPN, and which connect directly. That way, you can have secure connections without disrupting local device communications, slowing down your network, or losing access to essential tools.

[Get ExpressVPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)


## What split tunneling does and how it helps

Balance privacy, speed, and convenience with a split tunneling VPN


- Manage traffic between the VPN and local network
                                        
                                                                    
                        ![Fast And Reliable@2x 5](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/fast-and-reliable@2x-5.png)
                    
                                
                                                                [A VPN with advanced features](https://www.expressvpn.com/features) like split tunneling lets you choose which apps or traffic use the VPN and which stay on your regular internet. Many VPNs also offer an option to keep local network access enabled, so devices like printers or smart home gear remain reachable while selected apps stay protected.
- Improve app performance and bandwidth use
                                        
                                                                    
                        ![High Speed Servers@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/high-speed-servers@2x.png)
                    
                                
                                                                VPNs can sometimes slow your connection speeds because all traffic passes through an [encrypted tunnel](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/vpn-encryption), adding processing and longer server routes. High-bandwidth activities like streaming, gaming, or large downloads may be affected. This is where split tunneling steps in.
- Split tunneling vs. full tunneling explained simply
                                        
                                                                    
                        ![Split Tunneling@2x 3](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/split-tunneling@2x-3.png)
                    
                                
                                                                Unlike full tunneling, where all traffic routes through the VPN, split tunneling creates a dual-path setup. Full tunneling provides maximum protection but can slow down high-bandwidth activities. Split tunneling balances speed and security—your way.

## Frequently asked questions: Split tunneling in a VPN

What you need to know about a VPN with split tunneling


### What is VPN split tunneling, and how does it work?

VPN split tunneling lets you choose which apps or websites use the VPN and which connect directly to the internet. ExpressVPN keeps selected traffic encrypted while other connections stay normal, helping you balance privacy, speed, and connectivity across all devices.


### What is website-based split tunneling?

Website-based split tunneling lets you choose specific websites that bypass the VPN. To set this up, you’ll need the website’s IP address, which you can find using a website-to-IP lookup tool. ExpressVPN supports IPv4, IPv6, and subnet ranges for website-based routing, depending on the device you’re using.


### How does inverse split tunneling work?

While regular split tunneling allows you to pick which sites and apps bypass the VPN, inverse split tunneling lets you bypass the VPN by default. This means you can specify which sites or apps use the VPN.
On ExpressVPN desktop apps, set “All Other Apps” to bypass the VPN, then add the apps or IP addresses you want protected. This is useful when only certain traffic needs VPN protection, such as routing your browser through the VPN while leaving Spotify or gaming apps on your regular connection.


### Are there any security risks with split tunneling?

Excluding traffic from your VPN means that data not routed through ExpressVPN could be visible to your ISP or third parties. Always use VPN-protected connections for sensitive activities, downloads, or personal information to maintain privacy and security.


### Should I enable split tunneling?

Split tunneling can be useful if you want only certain apps or traffic to use the VPN while everything else connects normally. For example, you might protect your browser while letting video calls or local services use your regular connection. Sensitive activity should always stay VPN protected.


### How do I know if split tunneling is active?

Open the ExpressVPN app and check the Split Tunneling settings to review which apps or websites are configured to use the VPN or bypass it. If your custom selections appear, then split tunneling is enabled and managing traffic as configured.


### What’s the difference between full tunnel and split tunnel modes?

Full tunneling sends all traffic through the VPN. Split tunneling lets you choose which apps or websites use the VPN, while others connect directly. Inverse split tunneling protects only selected apps and sites, leaving the rest outside the VPN.


### Do all VPNs offer split tunneling?

No. Split tunneling support varies between VPN providers and devices. Some VPNs route all traffic through the VPN (full tunnel) with no split tunneling controls. Even when VPNs do offer split tunneling, it’s still usually not available in their iOS VPN apps. ExpressVPN supports split tunneling across multiple platforms, including iOS.


### What’s the best VPN for split tunneling?

ExpressVPN offers split tunneling across multiple platforms, including app-based, website-based, and even inverse split tunneling depending on the device. Combined with high-speed servers in 113 countries, a verified no-logs policy, and a 30-day money-back guarantee for first-time users, it gives you flexible control over how your VPN connection works.

# Threat Manager: Block app and site trackers with ExpressVPN

Prevent unwanted tracking across all your apps with automatic device-level protection


- Block trackers and malicious domains system-wide, not just in a browser
- Stop apps from sending behavioral data to third-party trackers
- Get layered protection and privacy with one ExpressVPN subscription

[Start Your Risk-Free Trial](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)

30-DAY MONEY-BACK GUARANTEE FOR FIRST-TIME USERS



## What Threat Manager blocks and why it matters

Block apps and websites from known malicious and tracking domains



### How trackers collect and use your data

Most apps and websites send data to multiple third-party trackers each time you use them. This can include what you view, how you interact with content, and details about your device and connection.

Advertising networks and analytic platforms use this data to build profiles for targeted advertising, personalization, and cross-app tracking. Your activity in one app can influence the ads and content you see everywhere else.



### Hidden risks in apps and websites

Most users don’t realize their browser is just a small part of their online activity. Fitness trackers, messaging apps, news readers, streaming apps, and mobile games routinely send data to third-party trackers to profile your behavior.

These connections continue even when apps aren’t actively in use, creating privacy gaps that a browser-only tracker blocker can’t address.



### How Threat Manager actively stops data from reaching trackers

Threat Manager runs on your device at the DNS level, intercepting connection requests before they leave.

When any app or service attempts to send data to a domain, Threat Manager checks it against regularly updated blocklists of known tracking and harmful domains. Blocklisted domains are prevented from receiving your data, stopping tracking at the source across all apps.



### Protects more than just your browser activity

Unlike browser extensions that only protect web traffic, Threat Manager blocks tracking across every installed application. Email clients, cloud storage apps, device utilities, and IoT companion apps all connect to analytics servers in the background.

Even when you're focused on one task, Threat Manager monitors and blocks dozens of background processes simultaneously.



### Stronger privacy alongside your VPN

[How does a VPN work](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn)? It encrypts your internet traffic, [masks your IP address](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-my-ip/hide-my-ip), and keep your activity hidden from ISPs, network administrators, and hackers on shared networks. But this protection doesn’t prevent apps and websites from sharing your behavioral data with third-party trackers.

The threat blocker adds a second layer of protection by preventing tracker connections at the device level, while your VPN secures the encrypted tunnel.

[Get ExpressVPN Threat Manager](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)



## How to turn on Threat Manager

Block trackers with Threat Manager in just one toggle



### Connect to the VPN using Lightway or ‘Automatic’ protocol

### Access ‘Advanced Protection’ in your app

Navigate to ‘Upgrades’ or ‘Preferences’, depending on your platform, to find ‘Advanced Protection’, where Threat Manager is located. The path may vary by device, but it is clearly labeled in your app settings.

[iOS](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-ios): Tap Add-ons > Advanced Protection

[Android](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-android): Tap Add-ons > Advanced Protection

### Toggle on ‘Block Trackers’ under ‘Advanced Protection’

On Linux, you can toggle on Threat Manager within the Terminal by running the following command:

For [Linux](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-linux), run these Terminal commands:


- To block trackers, enter the command: **expressvpn preferences set block_trackers true**
- To block malicious sites, enter the command: **expressvpn preferences set block_malicious true**

## FAQs: About Threat Manager

All you need to know about how to block trackers



### How is Threat Manager different from a DNS blocker?

Threat Manager is a DNS-based blocker that runs on your device rather than ExpressVPN's servers. This device-level architecture means blocked connection requests never leave your device and isn't logged anywhere. It covers all installed apps—not just browsers—and uses open-source blocklists updated weekly.


### Is Threat Manager free to use?

Threat Manager is included with your active ExpressVPN Advanced or Express Pro subscription. You can use it on iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, Linux, Apple TV (tracker blocker only), or on all devices connected to Aircove, ExpressVPN's Wi-Fi router.


### Is it safe to use Threat Manager all the time?

Yes! Threat Manager is built to run continuously (while the VPN is turned on), blocking trackers and malicious sites without affecting your device. Combined with ExpressVPN’s strict no-logs policy, it keeps your online activity private and secure whenever you’re connected.


### Can I use Threat Manager on my phone?

Yes. Threat Manager works on iOS and Android with Advanced or Express Pro subscriptions. Enable it under 'Advanced Protection' in the app, connect to the VPN using Lightway or Automatic protocol, and it will block trackers across all mobile apps and browsers.


### How do I stop websites and apps from tracking me?

Sign up with ExpressVPN and download the app for your device. Simply enable Threat Manager under ‘Advanced Protection’ on iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, or Linux.
If you’re using Aircove, Threat Manager is already set up with the router.


### Who uses trackers and why should I care?

Advertisers, analytics companies, and some apps use trackers to monitor your online activity. This data can be shared or sold to third parties to deliver targeted ads or personalized content, often without your knowledge. Threat Manager helps block these trackers, giving you greater control over your privacy.

[ExpressVPN’s ad-blocking feature](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/ad-blocker) helps you browse without annoying popups and banner ads cluttering your view.


- Password manager to secure your credentials

Safely access sensitive apps like Chase securely, whether you’re at home or on the go.
[ExpressKeys](https://www.expressvpn.com/keys) uses zero-knowledge encryption to store your credentials privately while autofill simplifies logins across devices.
- Parental controls for safer family internet use

You can customize settings per device, keeping your children’s tablets safe to use while your own connections remain unrestricted.
[Advanced Protection features](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/advanced-protection) include adjustable filters that block adult websites and unsafe domains.

## ExpressVPN works on all major devices

Protection that adapts to every gadget you rely on day-to-day



### iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, Linux, Fire TV, and more

With [native apps for PC](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-windows), mobile devices, and even [smart TVs](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-android-tv), you can protect every device you own from day one.

Enable auto-connect to launch protection as soon as you join any network, and shield your data while you’re on the move. [Split tunneling](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/split-tunneling) lets you customize connections and route your banking apps through the VPN for encryption while keeping local services like your news app on your regular connection.



### Use browser extensions on Chrome, Edge, and Firefox

Quick browser access lets you protect web traffic in a single click, preventing exposure to snoops or public Wi-Fi threats. You can run it independently without the desktop app or use it to control the app for device-wide protection.

ExpressVPN’s [extensions for Chrome](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/chrome-vpn) and other browsers are ideal for frequent travelers or shared workspaces. Built-in HTML5 location control and WebRTC blocking keep your actual IP address hidden while you browse or stream via your prefered subscriptions.


### Connect up to 14 devices simultaneously

A single plan supports [up to 14 active devices](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/how-many-devices-can-i-connect), enough to cover any gadget-lover or large household. Each connection benefits from strong encryption and stable speeds. The kids can stream, while your partner works, and you make money transfers safely, all at the same time.

Your device allowance remains the same, during the trial period and throughout your subscription.

## FAQ: About the ExpressVPN trial

Explore common questions about features, trials, and risk-free use



### Is there an ExpressVPN free trial?

Yes, ExpressVPN offers a 3-day free trial on iOS and Android when you sign up through the app. For new users who sign up directly through the website, ExpressVPN offers a 30-day money-back guarantee with the option to request a refund within 30 days.
Users who start their subscriptions through the iOS App Store or Google Play Store are not eligible for the ExpressVPN 30-day money-back guarantee.


### Can I start the free VPN trial without a credit card?

The ExpressVPN 3-day free trial is available on Android and iOS. To activate it, you’ll need a valid payment method linked to your App Store or Google Play account.


### Am I getting full access to all features with the trial VPN?

Yes, during the trial period, you can access and use all ExpressVPN features included with the subscription you select. Every plan includes 256-bit AES encryption, leak protection, a kill switch, and our speed-optimized Lightway protocol to keep your data secure and your connection fast.


### How many devices can I connect during the trial and on which platforms?

It depends on your subscription: Basic supports 10, Advanced supports 12, and Express Pro supports 14 simultaneous connections. Once you activate your free trial on iOS or Android, you can sign in on any supported platform, including Windows, macOS, Linux, and browser extensions, and use your full device allowance across them. Your connection limit is determined by your subscription tier and remains the same after the trial if you continue with a subscription.


### Is ExpressVPN safe to use and legal in my country?

VPN use is legal in most countries. Still, a few countries ban or limit VPN use, so it's best to familiarize yourself with the laws in your country. Plus, any illegal activity remains illegal, even with a VPN. Always use VPNs within legal limits.


### What is the difference between a free VPN trial and a money-back guarantee?

ExpressVPN offers a 3-day free trial when you sign up on iOS or Android. Subscriptions purchased through the iOS App Store or Google Play Store are not eligible for the 30-day money-back guarantee and are subject to the respective app stores' refund policies. New users who subscribe through the ExpressVPN website are eligible for the 30-day money-back guarantee.


### Will using the trial VPN slow down my internet or affect streaming quality?

ExpressVPN’s Lightway protocol and 10Gbps and 40Gbps servers are optimized for speed, supporting fast, stable streaming and browsing whether you connect to a nearby server or across the globe.


### How does ExpressVPN’s trial compare with a VPN free for iPhone?

Free VPNs often impose bandwidth limits, weaker encryption, and minimal privacy safeguards. ExpressVPN offers fast, stable connections, 256-bit AES encryption, and a verified no-logs policy. During your refund period, you experience the same premium protection trusted by millions, without the risks of free VPNs.


### How do I claim a refund or cancel if I decide not to keep it?

First-time ExpressVPN customers can request a refund within 30 days by contacting 24/7 live chat support (available in multiple languages). Subscriptions purchased through the iOS App Store or Google Play Store are not eligible for the 30-day money-back guarantee and are subject to the respective app stores' refund policies.

- Get instant access to VPN protection on all your devices
- Unlimited data, no logs—just private, secure internet
- Only available to new subscribers
- Request a refund via customer support within 30 days and get your money back

## Chrome VPN extension vs. VPN app: Which do you need?

An extension covers your browser; the full VPN protects your whole device

![Chrome Extension Vpn@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/chrome-extension-vpn@2x.png)


### Lightweight browser protection for daily use

You may want the main privacy benefits of a [VPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn) in Chrome, but with total ease-of-use, very little setup, and one-click protection.

With a light footprint, the ExpressVPN extension (in Proxy Mode) gives you browser-only encryption and [hides your IP address](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-my-ip/hide-my-ip) from websites and third parties. If your privacy needs start and end with the browser, this is enough.

![Kill Switch Split Tunneling@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/kill-switch-split-tunneling@2x.png)


### Full device coverage with the ExpressVPN app

With only the extension, your laptop or desktop PC is still sending out unsecured data via emails, banking, or delivery apps. Pair it with the ExpressVPN desktop app, and the extension enters Remote Control mode.

In this mode, it acts as a dashboard for the app, which encrypts your traffic device-wide. It also includes advanced features, like the [Internet Kill Switch](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/network-lock), which kicks in if your connection drops and pauses all traffic until the VPN tunnel is back up.

![Quick Private Connections@2x 4](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/quick-private-connections@2x-4.png)


### Use both for maximum security

Even if the desktop app secures your whole connection, your browser may still leak valuable data (like your physical location or IP address) in various ways. By pairing the ExpressVPN app with the Chrome extension, you cover your bases better.

The extension directly manages WebRTC blocking and HTML5 geo-spoofing within Chrome, while the app takes care of other leak-prevention methods. You browse with more protection from device and browser-based vulnerabilities—the best of both worlds.

[Download ExpressVPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)

## Frequently asked questions

Quick answers on Chrome’s built-in protections, free VPN extensions, and more


### Does Google Chrome have a built-in VPN?

No, it only has an HTTPS-only mode, not a real VPN in Google Chrome. If you’re comparing VPN extensions for Chrome or you're looking for the best VPN for Chrome, ExpressVPN adds IP masking, encrypted browser traffic, and global server choice.


### Is there a free VPN for Chrome that is safe?

Usually not. One free VPN was even caught silently taking screenshots of every page its 100,000+ users visited, including banking and personal data. ExpressVPN's Chrome extension gives you audited protection and a verified no-logs policy. Gambling on a free VPN for Chrome can cost you.


### How do I turn off my VPN in Chrome?

Click the ExpressVPN icon in your toolbar and hit Disconnect. If you’re figuring out how to change VPN locations in Chrome, the same control lets you switch servers or turn it off for specific tabs without affecting your VPN-secured tabs and downloads in Chrome.


### Will a Chrome VPN extension hide my IP address?

Yes. A VPN Chrome extension replaces your visible IP address with the server’s IP. Our VPN for Chrome also blocks WebRTC and HTML5 leaks that can expose your real IP address in the browser. If you’re learning how to use a VPN with Chrome safely, connect first, then confirm the visible IP matches the server you chose.

## Tips for using ExpressVPN effectively with Edge

- Enable auto-connect on browser launch

- Use Smart Location for fastest speeds

Connect with one click for fastest speeds. ExpressVPN automatically selects the optimal server using Smart Location. It analyzes real-time network conditions to deliver the best performance for your location.



- Access streaming content while traveling

Connect to a server in your home country to use your existing streaming subscriptions. Whether you're on a business trip or vacation, stay caught up on your favorite shows by connecting where your accounts are registered.
- Enable WebRTC blocking

Prevent websites from detecting your real IP address through WebRTC leaks. WebRTC can inadvertently reveal your true location, even when using a VPN. Enable this in extension settings to close the gap.

## Frequently asked questions

Everything you need to know about ExpressVPN for Edge



### Is the ExpressVPN Microsoft Edge extension safe?

Yes. The ExpressVPN Microsoft Edge browser extension can be used on its own or with the full-featured ExpressVPN app. With fast connection speeds, strong data encryption, and easy server switching, ExpressVPN offers a superior experience along with strong security. When used with the VPN app, you can access even more features like the Internet Kill Switch.


### Does a VPN hide my browser history?

Your browser stores history locally, which a VPN doesn’t change—you can clear it manually. ExpressVPN encrypts your internet traffic, preventing ISPs or Wi‑Fi operators from seeing your sites. Note: searches while logged into accounts (e.g., Google) may still be recorded separately.


### How do I use a VPN on Microsoft Edge?

Subscribe to ExpressVPN, download and install the extension, sign in, and connect to a server location. The extension works with the desktop app to secure your entire device while letting you control protection directly from your browser.


### Is the Microsoft Edge built-in VPN enough?

Microsoft Edge’s built-in VPN, the Edge Secure Network, provides basic protection for Edge users, but only encrypts traffic from the Edge browser and limits you to just 5GB of free data per month.


### Can I install ExpressVPN without the desktop app?

Yes. You can use the ExpressVPN browser extension on its own without installing the desktop app. Without the app, the extension works as a VPN proxy that protects only your browser traffic. For device-wide protection, you can also pair the extension with the ExpressVPN app, but this is optional.

## What is a Firefox VPN extension?

It adds encryption and IP masking directly to your Firefox browser traffic

![Dns Leak Protection@2x 4](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/dns-leak-protection@2x-4.png)


### What does a Firefox VPN extension do?

It encrypts your Firefox browser’s traffic to protect your privacy and lets you change your virtual location to browse from a server location of your choice.

Beyond encryption, some Firefox VPN extensions come with extra features that add more layers of privacy and security to your browsing.

![Quick Private Connections@2x 2](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/quick-private-connections@2x-2.png)


### VPN apps vs extensions: Key differences

A Firefox VPN extension typically covers only your browser’s traffic, while a VPN app encrypts traffic across your entire device.

ExpressVPN's Firefox extension can do both: use it as a standalone browser proxy, or pair it with the app for full-device encryption.

![Kill Switch Split Tunneling@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/kill-switch-split-tunneling@2x.png)


### Do you need a VPN app for full protection?

Not necessarily. If privacy while browsing is your main concern, the extension alone is enough.

If you want your entire device's traffic encrypted, including other apps and browsers, you'll want the full VPN app, with or without the extension.

[Download a Firefox VPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/pricing)


## Does Firefox have a built-in VPN?

Firefox launched a built-in privacy feature that masks your IP address, but it's not a VPN


- ![Leak Protection@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/leak-protection@2x.png)
                    
                                
                                                                        Firefox built-in VPN vs. browser extensions
                                                                                        Firefox's built-in feature is a proxy service, not a full VPN. It masks your IP address but relies on existing HTTPS encryption rather than adding a dedicated encryption layer.
A VPN browser extension, by contrast, adds a much stronger encryption layer on top and often comes with important protections, such as [DNS leak prevention](https://www.expressvpn.com/dns-leak-test), built in.
- ![Phone Laptop Vpn@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/phone-laptop-vpn@2x.png)
                    
                                
                                                                        Limitations of built-in options
                                                                                        Firefox's built-in option comes with notable restrictions: a 50GB monthly data cap, no server location choice, browser-only traffic coverage, and availability currently limited to the US, UK, Germany, and France.
A dedicated VPN extension typically has none of these constraints.

## Why you need a VPN for Firefox

A Firefox VPN boosts your security and helps avoid content-based ISP throttling

![Wifi Vpn](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/wifi-vpn.png)


### Protect your data on public Wi-Fi

On [public Wi-Fi](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/wifi-vpn), Firefox’s privacy features can't keep hackers on the same network from monitoring your online activity and intercepting unencrypted traffic.

ExpressVPN [encrypts your browser traffic](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/vpn-encryption) before it leaves your device, making it significantly harder for hackers to capture your data. This minimizes your risk when booking flights, sending emails to colleagues, or accessing your bank account on unsecured networks.

![Protect Your Right@2x 1](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/protect-your-right@2x-1.png)


### Hide your IP address and browsing activity

Without a VPN, websites you visit will see your real IP address, while your ISP or local network can see which sites you're visiting and when. They can use the information to profile you, track you, or determine your approximate location.

With ExpressVPN’s encryption, you can limit how much websites, your ISP, and local networks can see of your online activity. Together with Firefox’s ad tracker blocking and Private Browsing mode, you can go online with enhanced privacy.

![Slow Internet Speeds 1](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/slow-internet-speeds-1.png)


### Avoid content-based ISP throttling

You’re getting ready for movie night when your stream suddenly won’t stop buffering. There may be several reasons for this, one of which could be your [ISP throttling your streaming traffic](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/isp-throttling).

To get around this, connect to an ExpressVPN server before opening your favorite streaming app. This can help stop your ISP from detecting and slowing down specific traffic, like streaming (known as content-based throttling).

## Free vs. paid Firefox VPNs

Paid Firefox VPNs offer more ways to maintain your security and privacy


- ![Data Exposed@2x 1](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/data-exposed@2x-1.png)
                    
                                
                                                                        Free Firefox VPNs
                                                                                        Some free Firefox VPNs collect your activity and connection logs, and sell them to third parties. Others only offer a proxy service with no dedicated encryption layer like 256-bit AES, which can make your data significantly easier to intercept.
Even worse, some free VPNs use a peer-to-peer network model to save money. This routes your traffic through another VPN user’s device and vice versa, putting your privacy and security at risk.
- ![Multiple Devices@2x 4](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/05/multiple-devices@2x-4.png)
                    
                                
                                                                        Paid Firefox VPNs
                                                                                        Paid VPNs understand the value of [trust](https://www.expressvpn.com/trust). ExpressVPN, for example, keeps no connection or activity logs, and protects your traffic with industry-leading encryption.
One subscription also covers [up to 14 devices at once](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/how-many-devices-can-i-connect), and our apps come with features like our [Internet Kill Switch](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/network-lock), which keeps your data safe if your connection drops and goes online again before the VPN reconnects.

## Frequently asked questions

Your common questions about Firefox VPNs, answered


### Does Firefox have a built-in VPN?

Yes, Firefox launched its built-in VPN in March 2026. Initially, available to users in the US, the UK, Germany, and France, it masks your IP address, though server location is assigned automatically with no option to choose your own.


### How do I turn on VPN on Mozilla Firefox?

To use a VPN on Mozilla Firefox, install the ExpressVPN extension from the Firefox Add-ons store. Once installed, click the ExpressVPN icon in your toolbar, sign in, and connect to a server of your choice.


### What is the best VPN for Firefox?

ExpressVPN is the best Firefox VPN. Our browser extension can work as a standalone browser proxy with no app required, or as a remote control for the ExpressVPN desktop app, giving you full device protection without leaving Firefox.


### Is there a free VPN for Firefox?

Yes, but free Firefox VPNs typically come with significant limitations: fewer server locations, data caps, and weaker security standards. ExpressVPN offers servers across 113 countries, no data caps, and 256-bit AES encryption as a more complete alternative.


### Do I need a VPN app with a browser extension?

Having a VPN extension for Firefox installed along with an app isn't necessary but can be a convenient option. ExpressVPN, for example, lets you control the desktop app using the Firefox extension so you don't need to leave the browser window to manage your VPN settings.


### Is a Firefox VPN extension safe?

Yes, but it depends entirely on the provider. ExpressVPN proves its safety and security standards with regular independent audits through companies like KPMG and PwC. These auditors verify our systems work as promised, in line with our strong no-logs policy.

If your VPN doesn’t have fast servers near you, your connection starts dragging when browsing, streaming, or gaming. ExpressVPN runs 10Gbps and 40Gbps [servers across 214+ locations in 113 countries](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-server).

![No Logs](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/no-logs.png)


### Trusted no-logs policy

A top-tier VPN only earns the title if it protects your data on its own systems as well. ExpressVPN follows a strict [no-logs policy](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/policy-towards-logs), verified through regular independent audits by KPMG, PwC, and more.

Our [TrustedServer technology](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/trustedserver) runs on RAM-only servers. Nothing is ever written to a hard drive, and all remaining server data is gone the moment a server reboots.

![Fast And Reliable@2x 2](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/fast-and-reliable@2x-2.png)


### Lightway protocol

A mobile-first VPN has to deal with the way phones work in real life, including battery drain, network switching, and sudden drops. Our fast [Lightway protocol](https://www.expressvpn.com/lightway) does just that.

Its lean Rust codebase is more efficient than older protocols, so the VPN consumes less processing power and battery life. When you switch networks or lose signal, Lightway also reconnects fast so your protection picks back up with minimal disruption.

![Automatic Obfuscation@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/automatic-obfuscation@2x.png)


### Help with restrictive networks

Some networks can restrict your VPN activity via firewall blocks, which can make free VPN options for Android struggle. ExpressVPN uses automatic obfuscation on all servers, disguising VPN traffic as regular HTTPS web traffic.

This makes it much harder for networks to flag and block your VPN traffic. Your ExpressVPN connection on Android is more likely to work well in environments where other apps usually show cracks.

## Advanced privacy features for mobile

Protect data during connection drops, choose what the VPN covers, and more


- Internet Kill Switch and DNS leak protection
                                    
                
                                            ![Step 1](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/network-lock-mobile@2x.png)
                                    
                                
                                                                If your connection drops due to bad Wi-Fi or network switching, the encrypted tunnel “falls out”, leaving your data wide open. But our [Internet Kill Switch](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/network-lock) pauses all internet traffic on your Android device until the tunnel is back up. Plus, it covers IPv4, IPv6, and DNS requests simultaneously (protecting you from potential [DNS leaks](https://www.expressvpn.com/dns-leak-test)). Your activity stays protected during connection outages.
- Split tunneling: Choose which apps use the VPN
                                    
                
                                            ![Step 2](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/split-tunneling@2x.png)
                                    
                                
                                                                You may not need every app to send traffic inside the encryption tunnel: your banking app can stay protected while a delivery app uses your actual IP location. With ExpressVPN’s [split tunneling](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/split-tunneling) feature, you can choose which apps use the VPN and which connect directly. You get privacy where you want it while letting other apps run normally without affecting connection speeds or functionality.
- Dedicated IP option for consistent connectivity
                                    
                
                                            ![Step 3](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/fast-location@2x.png)
                                    
                                
                                                                Some apps and services (like secure business portals and gaming apps) expect a consistent IP address and may flag frequent changes. With a [dedicated IP address](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/dedicated-ip) add-on (included in Express Pro subscriptions), you get a VPN IP address that’s assigned only to you. Not even ExpressVPN can trace it back to you. You get more consistent access for services that require a fixed IP, without giving up the benefits of VPN. Dedicated IP servers are limited to specific locations.

## FAQ: VPN for Android

All of your burning questions about Android VPN functionality, answered


### Does Android have a built-in VPN?

Yes, Android includes built-in VPN support, but it isn’t a full VPN app for Android. You still need a VPN subscription. ExpressVPN gives you access to a global service, 24/7 support, and one-tap protection that make your Android VPN much easier (and safer) to use every day.


### Do I need a VPN if I only use mobile data?

Yes. On mobile data, your carrier can still see traffic patterns and apply content-based throttling. ExpressVPN encrypts your traffic, making your activity harder to inspect and helping you avoid content-based caps across both Wi-Fi and mobile networks.


### How does a VPN hide my data from my ISP?

A VPN encrypts your traffic before it leaves your phone, making it harder for your ISP to inspect what you do online. ExpressVPN uses 256-bit AES encryption, so your ISP can see you're using a VPN but can't see your specific activity.


### Can I use ExpressVPN on Android TV and tablets?

Yes. ExpressVPN works across Android phones, tablets, and Android TV, so one subscription covers more than a single screen. A single subscription protects up to 14 devices simultaneously, depending on tier, which is one reason many users see it as the best VPN for Android.


### Is it safe to use a VPN for mobile banking?

Yes, especially on public Wi-Fi, where snooping and interception are bigger risks. ExpressVPN puts your banking data inside an encrypted tunnel, giving you a safer way to handle sensitive activity on an Android phone.


### How do I set up and manage a VPN on Android?

Learning how to set up a VPN on Android or how to use a VPN on Android is simple: Just download ExpressVPN, sign in, and tap Connect. It's also easy to turn off the VPN from the app when you need to.


### Is a free VPN enough for Android?

Usually not. A free VPN for Android may come with weaker privacy, slower performance, or fewer protections than a paid option. ExpressVPN is built for people who want the best VPN for Android, not just the cheapest one.

### TrustedServer technology for privacy by design

ExpressVPN uses [TrustedServer technology](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/trustedserver) on our RAM-only servers to wipe all data with each reboot, so they can’t log and store your online activity. Each server loads a fresh software stack at startup to minimize potential vulnerabilities, too.

Both our TrustedServer technology and [no-logs policy](https://www.expressvpn.com/what-is-vpn/policy-towards-logs) undergo regular third-party audits by independent services to ensure what you watch on your Apple TV is truly kept private.



### Post-quantum protection to future-proof your security

Keep your Apple TV safe from attackers that have access to either classical or quantum computers (when those arrive).

We’ve integrated our Lightway protocol with [quantum-resistant](https://www.expressvpn.com/?p=2254793) algorithms to keep your encrypted traffic secure. Even as quantum computing advances in the next few years.



### 214+ server locations worldwide

Take control of your virtual location with ExpressVPN’s worldwide [server network](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-server). We refresh our server IP addresses regularly to maintain private and reliable connections for streaming on your Apple TV and beyond.

Each server is equipped with automatic obfuscation. That way, you can use your Apple TV VPN without anyone noticing, even when faced with network restrictions or firewall blocks.



### Works on up to 14 devices simultaneously

Worried about your other Apple gadgets? Or protecting a big household? We’ve got you covered with [native VPN apps](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download) for all Apple devices and support for up to [14 simultaneous device connections](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/how-many-devices-can-i-connect). Our support team is available 24/7 to help you, whether it’s giving setup instructions or tips on how to use a VPN with your Apple TV. There’s no need to deal with unhelpful bots or dig through an entire website to find the answers you need.

You’ll also find an exhaustive FAQ page on ExpressVPN’s website, as well as troubleshooting tips and setup guides in our [Support Center](https://www.expressvpn.com/support/). A search bar makes it easy to find what you’re looking for.



### Choose the VPN protocol that fits your needs

## FAQs: About Apple TV VPNs

Get answers to common Apple TV VPN questions



### Can I use a VPN with Apple TV?

Yes, you can. Using a VPN with your Apple TV secures all the traffic between your TV and the internet while streaming and listening to music. ExpressVPN offers a native Apple TV app for devices running tvOS 17 and later.


### Does Apple TV support VPN apps?

Yes. Apple TV devices running tvOS 17 or later support VPN apps, including ExpressVPN. Just search for ExpressVPN in the App Store, download the app, sign in, and connect. If your Apple TV is running tvOS or earlier, ExpressVPN offers alternative setup options like MediaStreamer.


### How do I use a VPN on Apple TV?

It's easy. Once the app is installed on your device, sign in, choose a server location, and connect. You can also save favorite locations for quicker access from the home screen, run a speed test to compare server performance, and switch between VPN protocols.


### How do I change my VPN location on Apple TV?

When you connect to a different VPN server, your Apple TV uses the IP address of that server instead of your IP address. Changing your VPN location depends on which setup method you use. For instance, if you use ExpressVPN's Apple TV app, all you need to do is pick a new server location in the app.
If you’re using the Aircove router, you can log in to [expressvpnrouter.com](https://www.google.com/url?q=http://expressvpnrouter.com&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1780492330649283&usg=AOvVaw0BnptIJoo_c7Fl3d8WyOap) and enter your details. From there, choose a new server and connect. For MediaStreamer setups, you’ll need to update your DNS settings in your ExpressVPN account dashboard and on your Apple TV.


### What do I do if Apple TV doesn’t allow VPN apps?

If your Apple TV model doesn’t support tvOS 17 or later, you can still use ExpressVPN with alternative setup methods.
You can connect your Apple TV through an Aircove router or share a VPN connection from your Windows PC. Another option is MediaStreamer, ExpressVPN’s smart DNS feature. It can improve your streaming experience, but it doesn’t encrypt your traffic or provide the privacy and security benefits of a VPN connection.


### Is it legal to use a VPN on Apple TV?

Yes, it's legal to use a VPN for Apple TV in most countries. There are only a few countries that ban VPNs due to strict legislation. So just be sure that you familiarize yourself with your local law and abide by the user agreements of the services you use through Apple TV.


### Can you use a free VPN for Apple TV?

There are free VPNs available for Apple TV, but they can't beat the reliability, security, speeds, and customer support of a paid VPN.
If you want to try a premium option first, ExpressVPN offers new subscribers a 30-day money-back guarantee, so you can try it with confidence. You’ll get access to the full Apple TV app, including multiple protocol options, built-in speed testing, and support for up to 14 simultaneous device connections.

## Why use a VPN on your Fire TV Stick?

Because no one else has to know what you’re watching



### Enhanced privacy on smart TVs

Smart TVs constantly collect data about what you watch and how you use apps—data that's often shared with advertisers and third parties.

While a [smart TV VPN](https://www.expressvpn.com/vpn-download/vpn-smart-tv) can't stop your Fire TV Stick from collecting this data, it encrypts your internet traffic so other third parties can’t monitor or log what you’re streaming.



### Safer streaming on public or shared networks

When you use a Fire TV Stick in a hotel or on shared Wi-Fi, other users or network operators could see which services you're accessing.

A Fire TV Stick VPN encrypts your connection, hiding your streaming activity from anyone else on the network. Whether you're at home or on public Wi-Fi, your connection stays private.


### Prevent bandwidth throttling

Internet providers routinely slow down high-bandwidth activity, like streaming. This causes streams to buffer and lowers the video quality.

With VPN encryption, your ISP can't see that you're streaming. Without this visibility, it can't selectively apply [ISP throttling](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/isp-throttling) to your connection, so you maintain consistent speeds.

Zero-knowledge encryption means only you can unlock your vault. Securely store passwords, cards, and notes, create strong logins, and spot exposed credentials.


### Leak protection and advanced security

DNS and IPv6 leaks can expose your browsing activity even when using a VPN, revealing the websites you visit despite encryption.

ExpressVPN’s [private DNS](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/dns) on every server keeps your activity private by handling DNS requests internally rather than through third-party services. This helps you avoid data logging by ISPs and DNS operators, as it prevents them from tracking which websites you visit.



### Browse, stream, and game with confidence

Are you tired of disruptive buffering while streaming on your iPhone or iPad? Some ISPs may deliberately slow down certain types of high-bandwidth traffic. ExpressVPN for iOS can help protect your activity with strong encryption, even from your ISP.

Your ISP can see you’re online, but not what you’re doing, [preventing content-based throttling](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/isp-throttling) and restoring consistent speeds. Plus, built-in obfuscation disguises VPN traffic as regular web activity, reducing the risk of interference.

## Linux distro compatibility

To run the Linux app (v5.0), your system must meet the following minimum requirements due to updated dependencies (e.g. glibc):



| **Distribution / Platform** | **Minimum Version** |
| --- | --- | 
| Ubuntu | 22.04 LTS or newer | 
| Debian | 11 (Bullseye) or newer | 
| Fedora | 35 or newer | 
| RHEL / CentOS | 9 or newer | 
| Linux Mint | 21 or newer | 
| Raspberry Pi OS | Bullseye or newer | 
| Arch (and derivatives) | 64-bit only; arm64 supported, but Pi requires 4K page size | 

⚠️ *If your distribution is older than the above, you will need to upgrade your OS before installing version 5.0.*

                                                            [TrustedServer](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/trustedserver) is ExpressVPN’s proprietary server technology, ensuring servers run exclusively on RAM and never write to disk. All server information is wiped on every reboot, so nothing you do online leaves a trace on our servers.
Our servers run on [fast speeds](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/fastest-vpn) and support high demand when streaming or browsing.
- ![Leak Protection@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/leak-protection@2x.png)
                            
                        
                        
                        
                                                                                                DNS leak protection
                                                            
                                                            DNS leaks can expose your online activity to others, including your ISP, hackers, and advertisers.
ExpressVPN uses its own [private DNS](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/dns), which encrypts and resolves your Mac’s DNS requests within our servers. This helps protect against DNS leaks, since it’s much harder for outsiders to snoop on your online activity.
- ![Internet Kill Switch@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/internet-kill-switch@2x.png)
                            
                        
                        
                        
                                                                                                Internet Kill Switch
                                                            
                                                            MacBooks’ long battery life make them ideal for working on the go. But they’re not immune to connection drops on spotty Wi-Fi, which can disconnect you from the VPN server and expose your traffic and IP address.
ExpressVPN’s [Internet Kill Switch](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/network-lock) pauses all internet traffic until your VPN connection returns, keeping your Mac’s data secure.
- ![Split Tunneling@2x 1](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/split-tunneling@2x-1.png)
                            
                        
                        
                        
                                                                                                Split tunneling
                                                            
                                                            Not every app needs the same level of protection. ExpressVPN's [split tunneling](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/split-tunneling) lets you choose which apps route through the VPN and which connect directly, so you stay protected where it matters without affecting everything else on your Mac.
- ![Secure Location@2x](https://www.expressvpn.com/wp-ws-cache/uploads-expressvpn/2026/06/secure-location@2x.png)
                            
                        
                        
                        
                                                                                                Dedicated IP option
                                                            
                                                            Some situations call for a consistent online identity, such as accessing your office network remotely or using your banking app while traveling.
ExpressVPN’s [dedicated IP](https://www.expressvpn.com/features/dedicated-ip) gives you a fixed address that’s exclusively yours across every session, so you can connect on your own terms whenever you want. It’s included with the Express Pro plan or available as an add-on.

Definitely. To use a VPN on your Mac, first subscribe to ExpressVPN, download and install the macOS app for your device, and sign in with your account to get started.
